tasks.title: Align GSD runtime to Node 22
tasks.summary: Local GSD scripts were aligned to Node 22 and the repository documentation now reflects the local install flow.
done_criteria: npm run gsd:* resolves to the installed local binary
Node 22+ is the documented runtime baseline
Contract tests enforce the package and README expectations

notes: postinstall hook executed successfully
GSD binary resolved locally from node_modules/.bin
GSD status query returned the expected snapshot
This task established the Node 22 baseline used by the later Tier 2 and Codex-first workflow work.

tasks.title: Force repo-local GSD home
tasks.summary: GSD runtime state was redirected to the repository-local .gsd directory so status and planning do not hit the read-only home sync path.
done_criteria: GSD commands use the repo-local GSD_HOME helper
The runtime no longer depends on the read-only global sync path
Status queries continue to work after install/reinstall

notes: Repo-local GSD_HOME helper was used by the runtime wrapper
Status queries no longer hit the previous EROFS sync failure
This task made GSD reproducible in this workspace and prevented future state writes from leaking to the host home directory.

tasks.title: Codex-first Superpowers + GSD workflow contract
tasks.summary: The repo's workflow contract now treats Codex as the runtime, Superpowers as process discipline, Context7 as evidence, and GSD as orchestration.
done_criteria: Repo docs separate runtime, process, evidence, and orchestration responsibilities
GSD preferences use OpenAI models and local skill routing
Superpowers skill names in wrappers match the installed skills

notes: The workflow contract now points at real installed Superpowers skills
GSD remained a routing/orchestration layer rather than a runtime replacement
This task is the canonical contract for future Codex-first work in this repo.

tasks.title: Create durable GSD task registry
tasks.summary: A writable task registry schema and JSON store were added under .gsd so future sessions can record completed work and read it before planning the next slice.
done_criteria: A JSON Schema exists for task records
A writable task registry exists under .gsd
GSD workflow docs read the registry after .gsd/STATE.md
Contract tests enforce the registry shape

notes: The registry file now records previously completed GSD slices and workflow contracts.
The registry contract is enforced by a dedicated Python test.
This entry makes the registry durable and self-describing so later sessions can reconstruct prior work quickly.

tasks.title: Complete Tier 2 dialogs and dock regressions
tasks.summary: Map Properties, Find Item, Waypoints, Layers, and the PropertiesDock regression guard were implemented and tested.
done_criteria: Map Properties loads and returns local state
Find Item supports local catalog search with list and grid modes
WaypointsDock keeps a local model with selection and editing actions
Layers toolbar exposes stable actions and defaults
PropertiesDock instantiates all value widgets

notes: Qt widget tests covered state round-trips and selection behavior
Layer toolbar defaults remained stable under regression test
This slice filled the Tier 2 UI parity gap for dialogs, docks, toolbar actions, and the PropertiesDock regression.

tasks.title: Normalize Tier 2 QSS and token contracts
tasks.summary: Shared stylesheet helpers now centralize Tier 2 dialog, dock, button, and item-view styling with alpha-safe color serialization.
done_criteria: Alpha-aware QColor serialization is used for translucent theme tokens
Shared QSS helpers are reused across Tier 2 dialogs and docks
The dock title and toolbar token contract matches DESIGN.md

notes: The new test suite confirmed alpha preservation and helper reuse
The full Python test suite remained green after the refactor
This slice removed repeated inline style blocks from the Tier 2 surface area.

tasks.title: Position + Tile PyO3 foundation
tasks.summary: Position and Tile were exposed through PyO3 with validated bounds and ordered stack semantics, then fully verified in the m2-position-tile worktree.
done_criteria: Position and Tile exist as PyO3 classes in the rme_core module
Tile preserves ordered item stack behavior
Python stubs and py.typed markers exist for the module

notes: Full Rust and Python verification passed in the feat-m2-position-tile worktree using the .venv-m2 Python 3.12 environment
Worktree closeout commit: 7f974f4
The canonical closeout path uses .venv-m2 with explicit PYO3_PYTHON and LD_LIBRARY_PATH so cargo test and Python integration tests run against the same Python 3.12 runtime.

tasks.title: Wire main window shell navigation state
tasks.summary: MainWindow now drives real shell navigation state, view visibility actions, and persisted layout without depending on the canvas backend.
done_criteria: Go To Position updates the active shell position and floor state
Floor actions and minimap Z controls mutate shell state instead of logging stubs
Dock and toolbar visibility actions are exposed through the View menu
Window geometry, layout, floor, and visibility toggles persist through QSettings

notes: The shell navigation slice was verified in the feat-m5-shell-navigation worktree
Worktree closeout commit: 28f458c
GSD status remained available, but headless new-milestone did not materialize milestone files in this environment
This slice keeps canvas rendering out of scope and uses QSettings plus QMainWindow state APIs to persist the editor shell.
The new shell tests intentionally avoid top-level show() calls to keep offscreen Qt verification stable.

tasks.title: Build live sprite draw plans from canvas frame data
tasks.summary: Canvas hosts now derive sprite draw plan diagnostics from the current CanvasFrame when fixture SpriteCatalog and SpriteAtlas inputs are provided, while explicit draw-plan injection remains a manual override.
done_criteria: Canvas hosts expose a fixture catalog and atlas input seam
Live inputs regenerate SpriteDrawPlan diagnostics from visible canvas frame tiles
Frame changes refresh sprite draw command diagnostics
Explicit set_sprite_draw_plan remains supported as an override
Failed sprite draw generation clears stale commands without discarding a valid frame plan
No pixel painting, GL upload, real asset loading, screenshots, lighting, or wgpu work is added
tests_run:
python3 - <<'PY'
import enum
import pytest
if not hasattr(enum, 'StrEnum'):
    class StrEnum(str, enum.Enum):
        pass
    enum.StrEnum = StrEnum
raise SystemExit(pytest.main([
    'tests/python/test_canvas_sprite_draw_diagnostics.py',
    'tests/python/test_canvas_seam_m4.py',
    'tests/python/test_renderer_frame_plan_integration.py',
    'tests/python/test_sprite_draw_commands.py',
    'tests/python/test_sprite_catalog_adapter.py',
    'tests/python/test_sprite_frame.py',
    'tests/python/test_render_frame_plan.py',
    'tests/python/test_diagnostic_tile_primitives.py',
    '-q',
    '--tb=short',
]))
PY

notes: Focused sprite draw diagnostics tests passed: 8 tests.
Adjacent canvas/render/sprite planning regression slice passed with the Python 3.10 StrEnum shim: 53 tests.
Ruff passed for the touched canvas host and tests.
git diff --check passed.
Live sprite draw planning uses fixture catalog and atlas inputs only.
Real asset provider ownership, atlas textures, pixel painting, screenshots, lighting, and wgpu remain future work.

tasks.title: Add sprite draw asset provider seam
tasks.summary: Canvas hosts now consume live sprite draw assets through a provider seam that supplies SpriteCatalog and SpriteAtlas inputs together, while direct fixture inputs and explicit draw-plan override behavior remain supported.
done_criteria: A renderer-facing sprite draw asset provider contract exists
A static provider exists for tests and fixture-backed flows
Canvas hosts accept a provider and use it for live sprite draw diagnostics
Provider inputs refresh when canvas frame synchronization runs
Direct fixture inputs and explicit draw-plan overrides remain supported
No pixel painting, GL upload, real asset loading, screenshots, lighting, or wgpu work is added

git diff --check passed.

tasks.title: Add fixture sprite draw asset bundle
tasks.summary: Sprite draw assets can now be grouped as a fixture bundle of DAT-like records, SPR-like frames, and atlas regions that implements the provider seam without file IO, pixel decoding, texture upload, or sprite painting.
done_criteria: SpriteDrawAssetBundle exists
build_sprite_draw_asset_bundle snapshots incoming iterables
The bundle implements sprite_draw_inputs
The bundle reuses existing catalog adapter and atlas region planner types
No real DAT/SPR file loading, pixel painting, GL upload, screenshots, lighting, or wgpu work is added

tasks.title: Discover client DAT and SPR asset paths
tasks.summary: Renderer asset discovery now resolves configured DAT/SPR files under a client root, falls back to Tibia.dat and Tibia.spr, and pairs discovery with fixture bundle providers without opening files.
done_criteria: ClientAssetFiles exists
discover_client_asset_files sanitizes configured file names to basenames
Configured metadata and sprite names are preferred before fallback names
Fallback names are Tibia.dat and Tibia.spr
Missing root, DAT, and SPR cases report legacy-style warnings
ClientSpriteAssetBundle pairs discovery results with an existing fixture bundle provider
No signature reads, binary parsing, pixel decoding, texture upload, sprite painting, screenshots, lighting, or wgpu work is added

tasks.title: Read client DAT and SPR signatures
tasks.summary: Renderer asset discovery now reads DAT/SPR signatures from discovered client files using legacy-style open/header warnings, without parsing records, decoding pixels, owning textures, or painting sprites.
done_criteria: ClientAssetSignatures exists
read_client_asset_signatures preserves discovery warnings when asset discovery is incomplete
Discovered DAT and SPR files are opened only to read the 4-byte signature header
DAT and SPR signatures are interpreted as little-endian unsigned values
Open failures and short headers report legacy-style warnings
No DAT item parsing, SPR frame parsing, pixel decoding, texture upload, sprite painting, screenshots, lighting, or wgpu work is added

tasks.title: Parse DAT item metadata
tasks.summary: Renderer asset parsing now reads DAT header and item sprite metadata into DatSpriteRecord rows with explicit DAT format flag remapping, without parsing SPR frames, decoding pixels, owning textures, or painting sprites.
done_criteria: DatItemMetadata exists
parse_dat_item_metadata parses DAT signature and item/creature/effect/distance counts
Item client ids from 100 through item_count produce DatSpriteRecord rows
Creature entries are consumed for stream alignment and excluded by default
Compact u16 and extended u32 sprite id widths are supported
Legacy DAT flags are remapped by explicit DAT format before payload skips
Invalid counts, zero dimensions, excessive sprite counts, unknown flags, and truncated data fail deterministically
No SPR frame parsing, pixel decoding, texture upload, sprite painting, screenshots, lighting, or wgpu work is added
tests_run: ../../.venv/bin/python3.12 -m ruff check pyrme/rendering/dat_item_metadata.py pyrme/rendering/sprite_draw_commands.py pyrme/rendering/__init__.py tests/python/test_dat_item_metadata.py

notes: Focused DAT metadata parser tests passed: 11 tests.

DAT format is explicit; automatic client-version detection remains future work.

tasks.title: Parse SPR frame table metadata
tasks.summary: Renderer asset parsing now reads SPR signature, sprite count, and archive offset table into SprFrameRecord rows with retained archive offsets, without decoding compressed pixels, owning textures, or painting sprites.
done_criteria: SprFrameMetadata exists
parse_spr_frame_metadata parses SPR signature and compact u16 sprite count
Extended u32 sprite counts are supported
One u32 archive offset is consumed for each sprite id starting at 1
Zero offsets are skipped as missing or empty sprite archive entries
Nonzero offsets produce SprFrameRecord rows with fixed 32x32 frame metadata
archive_offset is preserved in sprite catalog frame metadata
Excessive sprite counts and truncated offset tables fail deterministically
No compressed pixel reads, decompression, texture upload, sprite painting, screenshots, lighting, or wgpu work is added
tests_run: WSL: ../../.venv/bin/python3.12 -m ruff check pyrme/rendering/spr_frame_metadata.py pyrme/rendering/sprite_catalog_adapter.py pyrme/rendering/__init__.py tests/python/test_spr_frame_metadata.py tests/python/test_sprite_catalog_adapter.py
